title: Rosa × dumetorum pilosa
taxonomy: Rosa × dumetorum pilosa is a hybrid species of the Rosa genus. It is classified as a part of the family Rosaceae. Further, it is placed in the order Rosales, a group of flowering plants that also includes the rose family. This classification reflects its botanical relationship to other species in the same family. As a hybrid species, it is often studied in the context of plant evolution and taxonomy.
uses: Rosa × dumetorum pilosa, commonly known as the bristly wild rose, has been reported to have various medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat a range of ailments, including gastrointestinal issues and skin problems. Its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties are thought to contribute to its potential health benefits. Rosa × dumetorum pilosa has been used as a natural remedy for digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach cramps. However, its use is not widely studied or recommended by modern medical professionals.

constituents: Rosa × dumetorum pilosa, a species of rose, contains various active constituents that contribute to its medicinal properties. The primary active constituents of Rosa × dumetorum pilosa include flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, which possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The combination of these bioactive compounds in Rosa × dumetorum pilosa may contribute to its potential therapeutic applications. intro: Rosa × dumetorum pilosa, also known as the wild rose or Rosa rugosa, is a deciduous shrub native to East Asia, characterized by its fragrant pink flowers and edible hips. The health benefits of Rosa × dumetorum pilosa include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in relieving digestive issues, soothing skin irritations, and boosting the immune system. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Rosa × dumetorum pilosa is commonly used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as rosehip jam and syrup, which are valued for their nutritional and therapeutic value.
